records.ety: [L., fr. Gr. <?/; <?/ vegetable juice + pa`nax, pa`nakes, a kind of plant: cf. F. opopanax.]

defs: The inspissated juice of an umbelliferous plant (the Opoponax Chironum), brought from Turkey and the East Indies in loose granules, or sometimes in larger masses, of a reddish yellow color, with specks of white. It has a strong smell and acrid taste, and was formerly used in medicine as an emmenagogue and antispasmodic.
